heading: 2.3.7 Functions
text: mathematical operations operator. must use known function. example, take square root:function consists name function followed parentheses, list arguments inside parentheses. functions take one argument, take , take none .

heading: 2.4 Work from a script
text: point, running console typing commands console pressing enter.next section, run code script.Files tab (bottom right pane), click file named calculator.R.file open new pane called source pane upper left.can run code script placing cursor line clicking Run button source pane toolbar, using keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Enter / Cmd+Return.Let’s continue exploration functions R looking top script.commonly used function c(), combine function. combines multiple values ’s known vector. Place cursor line code run . cursor jump second line automatically run first one. cursor second line code, click Run run second line.functions take vectors arguments. example, length() function returns number elements, length, vector object.Useful mathematical functions include:can even nest functions. example , c() function evaluated first, value used argument length function.Combining several features together, can calculate standard error mean (SEM) confidence interval around mean set numbers:calculated SEM can use estimate upper lower limits 95% confidence interval like :
code:
k <- c(1, 2, 10, 4.7, 5.0)
k
#> [1]  1.0  2.0 10.0  4.7  5.0
length(k)
#> [1] 5
median(k)  # median value
#> [1] 4.7
mean(k)    # mean value
#> [1] 4.54
sd(k)      # standard deviation
#> [1] 3.501143
length(c(1, 2, 10, 4.7, 5.0)) # nested function
#> [1] 5
n <- length(k) # sample size
n
#> [1] 5
sem <- sd(k) / sqrt(n) # standard error of the mean
sem
#> [1] 1.565759
mean(k) + 1.96 * sem  # upper limit
#> [1] 7.608887
mean(k) - 1.96 * sem  # lower limit
#> [1] 1.471113
c(mean(k) + 1.96 * sem, mean(k) - 1.96 * sem)
#> [1] 7.608887 1.471113

heading: 3.4 Load packages
text: ggplot2 one core packages tidyverse. code , load ggplot2 package tidyverse packages. , get script nice start adding code section. Code sections keep code organized makes easy jump specific code sections without scrolling. long script, can real time saver. Code Sections chapter details, now can just follow instructions:Go Code > Insert Section… use keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+/ Cmd+Shift+IType “load packages” dialog window click OK buttonAs work tutorials, add code headings necessary.Load tidyverse package adding code new code section running :line code loads tidyverse package eight core packages within tidyverse, one ggplot2. vast majority data science needs can met eight packages. possible load package individually, example library(ggplot2), ’s usually better load tidyverse package often want use one packages tidyverse.run code, see message console like :first part tells packages loaded. notice ggplot2 list. second part message tells tidyverse functions conflict (override) functions. Usually functions base R, also functions packages already loaded. ever want use package overridden another package, refer explicitly package function name.
code:
library(tidyverse)#> ── Attaching packages ─────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse 1.3.0 ──
#> ✔ ggplot2 3.3.3     ✔ purrr   0.3.4
#> ✔ tibble  3.0.5     ✔ dplyr   1.0.3
#> ✔ tidyr   1.1.2     ✔ stringr 1.4.0
#> ✔ readr   1.4.0     ✔ forcats 0.5.0
#> ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
#> ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
#> ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()

heading: 3.5.1 Example: tiger data
text: use data Example 2.2A textbook activity. summary textbook follows:Conflict humans tigers threatens tiger populations, kills people, reduces public support conservation. Gurung et al. (2008) investigated causes human deaths tigers near protected area Chitwan National Park, Nepal. Eighty-eight people killed 36 individual tigers 1979 2006, mainly within 1 km park edge. Table 2.2-1 lists main activities people time killed. information may helpful identify activities increase vulnerability attack.dataset consists one table showing activities 88 people time attacked killed tigers near Chitwan National Park, Nepal, 1979 2006.can view explore data using interactive table:row table represents sample unit, person. Thus sample size 88.variables :person. number 1 88 identifying person data pertains . variable contains number, incorrect call numeric variable. number measured, mathematical functions applied calculating mean. value really different person’s name, consist text. Therefore best consider person categorical variable.person. number 1 88 identifying person data pertains . variable contains number, incorrect call numeric variable. number measured, mathematical functions applied calculating mean. value really different person’s name, consist text. Therefore best consider person categorical variable.activity. person’s activity time attacked killed. categorical variable discrete number possible values.activity. person’s activity time attacked killed. categorical variable discrete number possible values.purposes, variable interest activity. use person variable.

heading: 3.5.2 Read the tiger data
text: next step R script read data R.First, create new code section named “deaths tigers”data examples textbook can found book website. stored comma-separated values file (.csv).Read data textbook website using read_csv() function, part readr package, assign object named tiger_data using left assignment operator <- :message get console tells data CSV parsed (interpretted) read R. can ignore now.
code:
tiger_data <- read_csv("https://whitlockschluter.zoology.ubc.ca/wp-content/data/chapter02/chap02e2aDeathsFromTigers.csv")
#> 
#> ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#> cols(
#>   person = col_double(),
#>   activity = col_character()
#> )

heading: 3.5.3 View the tiger data
text: notice data appear console. ’s assigned object print . print data console, type name object R script run :output console indicates object tibble (tidyverse name table data) 88 observations (rows) 2 variables (columns).can view list distinct values taken categorical variable using distinct() function, returns table distinct values taken variable. first argument function name data, second argument name variable:

tiger_data                  # print the data in the console
#> # A tibble: 88 x 2
#>   person activity             
#>    <dbl> <chr>                
#> 1      1 Disturbing tiger kill

#> 5      5 Grass/fodder

heading: 3.5.4 Contingency table
text: want see many times value activity occurs data, can create contingency table using count() function. , data object first argument variable name second argument.can see, function returns another tibble two variables: original one activity new one n, represents number times variable occurred data.
code:
count(tiger_data, activity)
#> # A tibble: 9 x 2
#>   activity                  n
#> * <chr>                 <int>
#> 1 Disturbing tiger kill     5
#> 2 Fishing                   8
#> 3 Forest products          11
#> 4 Fuelwood/timber           5
#> 5 Grass/fodder             44
#> 6 Herding                   7
#> # … with 3 more rows

heading: 3.5.5 Make a bar graph
text:
distribution single categorical variable best visualized using bar graph (also called bar chart, barchart, column graph). section, learn create bar graph.ggplot uses layered approach building graph. learn means build first graph using tiger data. goal create bar graph similar one textbook Figure 2.2-1, looks like :first step create new ggplot using ggplot() function. first (now, ) argument data argument. set data object created . Now run code:plot something RStudio (.e. create graph), appear Plots tab lower right pane.Look Plots tab. see? first may think blank screen, actually gray box representing empty plot. point, R knows want plot tiger data, doesn’t know variables plot display data.display data, need add geometric object. case, want bar graph, use geom_bar() function. add geometric object canvas using plus sign like :first (now, ) argument geom_bar() mapping, aesthetic mapping tells R variables data table map parts graph. aesthetic mapping defined aes() function. geom_bar() requires one aesthetic, argument x, tells R variable put x axis. want put activity variable x axis, set x = activity . Put together, way add bars graph geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = activity)result figure, shown , look similar one Figure 2.2-1 textbook. couple notable exceptions:columns ordered heightThe columns ordered heightThe color redThe color redThe axis labels “count” “activity” changed something informativeThe axis labels “count” “activity” changed something informativeVarious theme elements improved:
font size larger
axis labels bold
axis tick labels (activity names) rotated 45 degrees
axis ticks removed
Various theme elements improved:font size largerThe axis labels boldThe axis tick labels (activity names) rotated 45 degreesThe axis ticks removedLet’s fix issues turn.First, arrange levels (categories) activity descending order count (.e. big columns left), using bit wizardry forcats package, one core packages tidyverse loaded loaded tidyverse package. fct_infreq() function changes variable factor levels defined frequently occur data. Inside aes() function, change x = activity x = fct_infreq(activity).Second, change color columns adding fill argument geom_bar(). Put comma closing parenthesis aes() function add fill =. fill color, try "red" "darkred", exact match use hex color "#C5351B".Third, add better x- y-axis labels using labs() function. Axis labels start capital letters , numeric variables, include units measurement parentheses. argument x sets x-axis label argument y sets y-axis label. label text appear quotation marks. x y arguments separated comma.Fourth, fix look graph using:width argument geom_bar() set make bars (columns) bit narrowerthe width argument geom_bar() set make bars (columns) bit narrowerscale_y_continuous() set y-axis limits 0 50, bufferscale_y_continuous() set y-axis limits 0 50, buffertheme_classic() remove background color, adds axis lines, increases base font sizetheme_classic() remove background color, adds axis lines, increases base font sizetheme() change various theme elements :
Make axis labels bold
Make x- y-axis text black larger
Make x-axis text angled right justified
Remove x-axis tick marks
theme() change various theme elements :Make axis labels boldMake axis labels boldMake x- y-axis text black largerMake x- y-axis text black largerMake x-axis text angled right justifiedMake x-axis text angled right justifiedRemove x-axis tick marksRemove x-axis tick marksCompare figure original . bad! one, however, can inserted document size resolution. importantly, code documents created graph, ensuring figure reproduced anyone.Note get pretty close just first three lines code perhaps one theme element rotate axis text labels.
code:
ggplot(data = tiger_data)
ggplot(data = tiger_data) +
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = activity))
ggplot(data = tiger_data) +
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = fct_infreq(activity)))
ggplot(data = tiger_data) +
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = fct_infreq(activity)), fill = "#C5351B")
ggplot(data = tiger_data) +
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = fct_infreq(activity)), fill = "#C5351B") +
  labs(x = "Activity", y = "Frequency (number of people)")
ggplot(data = tiger_data) +
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = fct_infreq(activity)), fill = "#C5351B", 
           width = .8) +
  labs(x = "Activity", y = "Frequency (number of people)") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 50), expand = expansion(mult = 0)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = rel(1)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
  )

heading: 3.6 Histograms
text: Histograms used show distribution single numerical variable.Now know basics creating ggplot, creating histogram require relatively little explanation.

heading: 3.6.1 Example: bird data
text: use data Example 2.2B textbook activity. summary textbook follows:many species common many rare? One way address question construct frequency distribution species abundance. data Table 2.2-2 survey breeding birds Organ Pipe Cactus National Monument southern Arizona, USA. measurements extracted North American Breeding Bird Survey, continent-wide data set estimated bird numbers (Sauer et al. 2003)dataset consists one table showing abundance 43 species Organ Pipe National. Monument.can view explore data using interactive table:row table represents sample unit, person. Thus sample size 88.variables :species. name bird species, categorical variable.species. name bird species, categorical variable.abundance. species’s abundance, numerical variable consisting positive integers.abundance. species’s abundance, numerical variable consisting positive integers.purposes, variable interest abundance. use species variable.

heading: 3.6.2 Read and view the bird data
text: Create new code section script named “bird abundances” read data textbook website:output console indicates object tibble 43 observations (rows) 2 variables (columns): species abundance.variable abundance numerical, categorical, help view distinct values, create contingency table showing number times value occurs.let’s get straight histogram.
code:
bird_data <- read_csv("https://whitlockschluter.zoology.ubc.ca/wp-content/data/chapter02/chap02e2bDesertBirdAbundance.csv")
#> 
#> ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#> cols(
#>   species = col_character(),
#>   abundance = col_double()
#> )

bird_data                  # print the data in the console
#> # A tibble: 43 x 2
#>   species          abundance
#>   <chr>                <dbl>
#> 1 Black Vulture           64
#> 2 Turkey Vulture          23
#> 3 Harris's Hawk            3
#> 4 Red-tailed Hawk         16
#> 5 American Kestrel         7
#> 6 Gambel's Quail         148
#> # … with 37 more rows

heading: 3.6.3 Make a histogram
text: Like ggplots, histogram begins blank canvas. Notice set data argument bird_data time rather tiger_data.used geom_bar() create bar graph, use geom_histogram() create histogram. aesthetic mapping, however, . require map x argument, .e. tell R variable x-axis. interested looking distribution abundance, set x argument abundance:see message console warning R used default number bins, 30. one textbook uses bins fall number 0, 50, 100, etc. Let’s try setting bin width instead number bins:number bins looks better, appears first bin centered around zero rather starting zero. can fix boundary argument, tells R one bin boundaries (boundaries can inferred bin width also set). Thus boundaries 0, 50, 100, etc.looks better, quite right. look raw data, see one data point Gila woodpecker abundance 300. Looking figure, ’s obvious value included bin left 300 rather one right. words, bins 250-299, 300-349. really want 250-300 301-350.Set closed argument "left" change left edge bin included bin, right edge. quick-minded might wonder makes first bin (includes zero 50) wider others. short, don’t know doesn’t matter species dataset abundance zero.finally, clean axis labels styling changes:
code:
ggplot(data = bird_data)
ggplot(data = bird_data) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = abundance))
#> `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.
ggplot(data = bird_data) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = abundance), binwidth = 50)
ggplot(data = bird_data) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = abundance), binwidth = 50,
                 boundary = 0, closed = "left")
ggplot(data = bird_data) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = abundance), binwidth = 50,
                 boundary = 0, closed = "left")
ggplot(data = bird_data) +
  geom_histogram(mapping = aes(x = abundance), binwidth = 50,
                 boundary = 0, closed = "left", 
                 fill = "#C5351B", color = "black") +
  labs(x = "Abundance", y = "Frequency (number of species)") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 30, 5), limits = c(0, 30), 
                     expand = expansion(mult = 0)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 600, 100)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = rel(1))
  )

heading: 9 Project Options
text: RStudio project consists , minimum, directory (aka folder) RStudio Project file. file name Project directory ends .RProj.One main purposes RProj file store Project Settings.can see Project Settings two ways:Go Tools > Project Options…Go Tools > Project Options…Files tab, click RProj fileIn Files tab, click RProj fileBoth methods cause dialog window pop showing project options.Project Options dialog

heading: 9.0.1 Suggested changes to default settings
text:
main thing change General settings avoid loading saving Project Workspace avoid saving History.Change project settings follows:Open Project Options dialogOpen Project Options dialogSelect General tab leftSelect General tab leftChange following settings (Default) 
Restore .RData workspace startup
Save workspace .RData exit
Always save History (even saving .RData)
Change following settings (Default) NoRestore .RData workspace startupRestore .RData workspace startupSave workspace .RData exitSave workspace .RData exitAlways save History (even saving .RData)Always save History (even saving .RData)Click OK buttonClick OK buttonNote: may also wish change Global Settings way. still important, however change project settings.Project version control (using Git), .RProj file now show modified. commit changes , repository also GitHub, push commit. Don’t forget give useful commit message like “Change project general settings default ”.

heading: 10 Code Sections
text: RStudio provides useful tool called code sections.code section special comment ends least four trailing dashes (-), pound signs (#), equal signs (=).Code sections three main uses:visual separate identify major sections code, making script readable.visual separate identify major sections code, making script readable.used create Document Outline. can jump directly code section clicking name Document Outline.used create Document Outline. can jump directly code section clicking name Document Outline.can jump directly section using Jump menu bottom left corner source pane.can jump directly section using Jump menu bottom left corner source pane.screenshot code sections action:Screenshot script three code sections showing Document OutlineAnd code script shown screenshot :
code:
## This script shows an example of using comments to create a document outline

# load packages -----------------------------------------------------------

library(tidyverse)


# read data ---------------------------------------------------------------

data <- read_xl("data.csv")


# plot data --------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot(data) +
  geom_point(aes(x, y))

heading: 10.1 Insert a code section
text: insert code section R script, one following:Use Insert Section… dialog Code menuUse keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+R / Cmd+Shift+RAdd four dashes (-) pound signs (#) end commentFor readability, add blank line code section comment

heading: 10.2 Show/hide the Document Outline
text: document outline appears right side source paneTo toggle Document Outline , one following:Click Document Outline button right side source pane toolbar. looks like four offset horizontal linesUse keyboard Shortcut Ctrl-Shift+O / Cmd+Shift+O

heading: 10.3 Jump to a code section
text: can jump code section quickly one following:Show Document Outline click headingClick Jump menu select headingAs scripts become longer, code sections become useful.
